steps.title: Find the common factor
steps.content: Each term in $19x^3+3x^2+8x$ contains a factor of $x$. Factoring out the greatest common factor means dividing every term by $x$ and keeping what remains inside parentheses.

steps.title: Divide each term
steps.content: Divide term by term: $19x^3\div x=19x^2$, $3x^2\div x=3x$, and $8x\div x=8$. The remaining terms become the terms inside the parentheses.

steps.title: Check by expanding
steps.content: Multiplying the factored form back out returns the same three terms in the original expression: $x(19x^2+3x+8)=19x^3+3x^2+8x$. The answer is C.
